typecheck.summary: TypeScript completed without errors after adding club logo state/API wiring, new club season migration, and upcoming-match error fallback.

lint.summary: ESLint passed for the updated team emblem/header/locker room UI, club admin service, API route, stores, and tests.

targetedTests.summary: Focused tests passed: 69 tests passed, covering club logo service authorization, team management upload UI, app logo state updates, and upcoming-match user-facing error fallback.

localSupabaseApi.summary: Local Supabase reset, QA auth seed, demo seed, and real API tests passed: 15 tests passed, including new club creation with default active season.

productionSupabase.command: apply migration new_club_season_and_logo_upload, then read-only SQL verification
productionSupabase.summary: Production now has matches red/blue leader confirmation columns, club-logos public bucket with 2MB image MIME limits, and the newly created 오뎅바 club has one active season; upcoming matches select returned an empty result instead of a schema error.

browserRuntime.command: Opened http://localhost:3000/ against the existing local Next dev server after local Supabase reset
browserRuntime.summary: First screen rendered with the local QA login view, no visible fatal error, no browser console errors after reload, and server log showed no new app error after the logo URL fix.

baseline.summary: Harness validation, lint, typecheck, and full Vitest suite passed: 19 test files passed, 1 skipped; 222 tests passed, 15 skipped.

issuesObserved: Production was missing the match lineup confirmation columns that current code selects.
Newly created clubs had no active season, which would block subsequent schedule creation.
The data model already had clubs.logo_url but no upload endpoint, storage bucket, or app state path to render it.
harnessImprovementProposal: No harness promotion proposed; production drift was corrected with a tracked migration and production read-only verification evidence.
retrospective.reason: The issue was a product/schema stabilization change, not a reusable harness rule.
